title: Russia's 'Mnogotochie': The anti-drone bullet that splits into three after firing
description: Russia is supplying its forces with new Mnogotochie ammunition for drone interception. This specialized round splits into three projectiles after firing to widen the engagement pattern. The ammunition is compatible with standard Russian small arms and requires no modifications. It is available in two calibres and effective up to 300 meters against drones. This development is part of a wider effort to counter unmanned aerial systems on the battlefield.

author: TOI WORLD DESK
title: From Texas to California, US data centre protests go nationwide amid AI infrastructure boom

This movement cuts across political party lines, demanding greater transparency.

author: TOI WORLD DESK
title: US military death toll in Iran war rises to 16 after two troops killed in Jordan
description: Two additional US service members died defending against Iranian attacks in Jordan.  This brings the total American military deaths in the conflict to sixteen.  The ongoing conflict is largely fought through drones and missile strikes.  Iranian authorities reported at least fifty people killed and over five hundred injured.  Civilians and foreign workers across the region have also suffered casualties.

author: AMIT ANAND CHOUDHARY
title: Using abusive language, swearing don't count as obscenity under IPC: Supreme Court
description: Supreme Court clarified that abusive language is not obscenity under law.  Mere swear words and profanities do not constitute an offense.  The court set aside a conviction for using abusive slurs during an altercation.  Obscenity requires a lascivious element appealing to prurient interests.  Such words must also cause annoyance to others in public places.

author: SWATI BHARADWAJ
title: Despite 35-minute delay, Vikram-1 keeps date with Indian space history with Mission Aagaman success
description: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully launched into low Earth orbit on Saturday.  This historic mission placed six payloads into their targeted orbit successfully.  India now becomes the third nation with private orbital launch capability.  The Hyderabad-based startup achieved this significant milestone on its first attempt.  This launch signifies a new era for private Indian space industry operations.

title: Medical colleges to decide own faculty appointments, promotions: National Medical Commission

This change aims to improve institutional accountability and reduce unnecessary referrals. Applicants dissatisfied with decisions can seek a review within thirty days. The regulator will now focus on exceptional cases involving regulatory ambiguity.
